There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[goddess|kami]] of [[Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}

{{Short description|Japanese god}} {{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=July 2019}} {{Cleanup rewrite|date=December 2023}} }} __FORCETOC__ {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Konohana Sakuya]] | siblings = [[Kaya-no-hime]] | caption = [[Kamiumi]] from the [[Kojiki]] | image = Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami-eng.svg | greek_equivalent = [[Ourea]] }} '''Ōyama-tsumi''' or '''Ohoyama-tsumi''' ([[Kojiki]]: {{lang|ja|大山津見神}} or [[Nihon Shoki]]: {{lang|ja|大山祇神}}, {{lang|ja|大山積神}}, {{lang|ja|大山罪神}}), also '''Ōyama-tsumi-mi'oya-no-mikoto''' ({{lang|ja|大山祇御祖命}}), is a god of mountains, sea, and war in [[Japanese mythology]]. He is an elder brother of [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. His other names are '''Watashi-no-Ōkami''' ({{lang|ja|和多志大神}}) and '''Sakatoke''' ({{lang|ja|酒解神}}).
== Genealogy == In the [[Kamiumi]] of the [[Kojiki]], Ōyama-tsumi was born between [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. After which he gave birth with '''[[Kaya-no-hime]]''' (鹿屋野比売神), also known as '''No-zuchi''' (野椎神), female deity from their union, the following gods pairs of eight were born:
* '''Ame-no-sazuchi''' (天之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sazuchi''' (国之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-sagiri''' (天之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sagiri''' (国之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-kurado''' (天之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-kurado''' (国之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ohoto-mato-hiko''' (大戸惑子神), male deity * '''Ohoto-mato-hime''' (大戸惑女神), female deity
Other children of Ōyama-tsumi who are parentless without a mother:
* '''Kamu'ō-ichi-hime''' (神大市比売), her children:
# '''Ōtoshi''' (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) or Nigihayahi (饒速日尊 Nigihayahi-no-mikoto), commonly known: '''[[Toshigami]]''' (年神) or Ōtoshi (大年神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) # '''[[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂神, Uka-no-mitama-no-kami), commonly known as [[Inari Ōkami|'''Inari''']] (稲荷神, Inari-no-kami)
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Konohanachiru-hime|lt='''Ko-no-hana-chiru-hime'''|ja|木花知流比売}} (木花知流比売) - wife of Yashimajinumi (八島士奴美神), his nephew through Susanoo * '''[[Iwanaga-hime]]''' (石長比売) or (磐長姫命 in the [[Kujiki|''Kujiki'' (旧事紀), or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (先代旧事本紀)]]) - sister of Ko-no-sakuya-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] * '''Ko-no-sakuya-hime''' (木花之佐久夜毘売) - sister of Iwanaga-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]
including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[goddess|kami]] of [[Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}

* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[goddess|kami]] of [[Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Izumo generations == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese god}} {{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=July 2019}} {{Cleanup rewrite|date=December 2023}} }} __FORCETOC__ {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Konohana Sakuya]] | siblings = [[Kaya-no-hime]] | caption = [[Kamiumi]] from the [[Kojiki]] | image = Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami-eng.svg | greek_equivalent = [[Ourea]] }} '''Ōyama-tsumi''' or '''Ohoyama-tsumi''' ([[Kojiki]]: {{lang|ja|大山津見神}} or [[Nihon Shoki]]: {{lang|ja|大山祇神}}, {{lang|ja|大山積神}}, {{lang|ja|大山罪神}}), also '''Ōyama-tsumi-mi'oya-no-mikoto''' ({{lang|ja|大山祇御祖命}}), is a god of mountains, sea, and war in [[Japanese mythology]]. He is an elder brother of [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. His other names are '''Watashi-no-Ōkami''' ({{lang|ja|和多志大神}}) and '''Sakatoke''' ({{lang|ja|酒解神}}).
== Genealogy == In the [[Kamiumi]] of the [[Kojiki]], Ōyama-tsumi was born between [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. After which he gave birth with '''[[Kaya-no-hime]]''' (鹿屋野比売神), also known as '''No-zuchi''' (野椎神), female deity from their union, the following gods pairs of eight were born:
* '''Ame-no-sazuchi''' (天之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sazuchi''' (国之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-sagiri''' (天之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sagiri''' (国之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-kurado''' (天之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-kurado''' (国之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ohoto-mato-hiko''' (大戸惑子神), male deity * '''Ohoto-mato-hime''' (大戸惑女神), female deity
Other children of Ōyama-tsumi who are parentless without a mother:
* '''Kamu'ō-ichi-hime''' (神大市比売), her children:
# '''Ōtoshi''' (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) or Nigihayahi (饒速日尊 Nigihayahi-no-mikoto), commonly known: '''[[Toshigami]]''' (年神) or Ōtoshi (大年神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) # '''[[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂神, Uka-no-mitama-no-kami), commonly known as [[Inari Ōkami|'''Inari''']] (稲荷神, Inari-no-kami)
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Konohanachiru-hime|lt='''Ko-no-hana-chiru-hime'''|ja|木花知流比売}} (木花知流比売) - wife of Yashimajinumi (八島士奴美神), his nephew through Susanoo * '''[[Iwanaga-hime]]''' (石長比売) or (磐長姫命 in the [[Kujiki|''Kujiki'' (旧事紀), or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (先代旧事本紀)]]) - sister of Ko-no-sakuya-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] * '''Ko-no-sakuya-hime''' (木花之佐久夜毘売) - sister of Iwanaga-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]
including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|12=draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[goddess|kami]] of [[Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Ooyamatsimu]][[commons:Category:Ōyamatsumi]][[de:Ōyamatsumi]][[en:Ōyamatsumi]][[fa:اویاماتسومی]][[fr:Ōyamatsumi]][[hr:O-Yama-Tsu-mi]][[ja:オオヤマツミ]][[ko:오야마쓰미노카미]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ]][[ru:Ооямацуми]][[simple:Ōyamatsumi]][[sv:Ōyamatsumi]][[zh:大山津見]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386563]] Wikidata name: Ōyamatsumi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese god}} {{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=July 2019}} {{Cleanup rewrite|date=December 2023}} }} __FORCETOC__ {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[:en:Konohana Sakuya|Konohana Sakuya]] | siblings = [[Kaya-no-hime]] | caption = [[Kamiumi]] from the [[Kojiki]] | image =File:Ooyamatsumi shinbutsu-zue.jpg| greek_equivalent = [[:en:Ourea|Ourea]] | gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}} | parents ={{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306}}| consort = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|qid=Q8191878}} | offspring = {{ill|Iwanaga|qid=Q8191705}}; {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|qid=Q11588867}}; {{ill|Konohanachiruhime|qid=Q48745675}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862}} | venerated_in = {{ill|Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qid=Q127948103}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|mountain|qid=Q8502}} }} '''Ōyama-tsumi''' or '''Ohoyama-tsumi''' ([[Kojiki]]: {{lang|ja|大山津見神}} or [[Nihon Shoki]]: {{lang|ja|大山祇神}}, {{lang|ja|大山積神}}, {{lang|ja|大山罪神}}), also '''Ōyama-tsumi-mi'oya-no-mikoto''' ({{lang|ja|大山祇御祖命}}), is a god of mountains, sea, and war in [[Japanese mythology]]. He is an elder brother of [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. His other names are '''Watashi-no-Ōkami''' ({{lang|ja|和多志大神}}) and '''Sakatoke''' ({{lang|ja|酒解神}}).
== Genealogy == In the [[Kamiumi]] of the [[Kojiki]], Ōyama-tsumi was born between [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. After which he gave birth with '''[[Kaya-no-hime]]''' (鹿屋野比売神), also known as '''No-zuchi''' (野椎神), female deity from their union, the following gods pairs of eight were born:
* '''Ame-no-sazuchi''' (天之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sazuchi''' (国之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-sagiri''' (天之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sagiri''' (国之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-kurado''' (天之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-kurado''' (国之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ohoto-mato-hiko''' (大戸惑子神), male deity * '''Ohoto-mato-hime''' (大戸惑女神), female deity
Other children of Ōyama-tsumi who are parentless without a mother:
* '''Kamu'ō-ichi-hime''' (神大市比売), her children:
# '''Ōtoshi''' (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) or Nigihayahi (饒速日尊 Nigihayahi-no-mikoto), commonly known: '''[[Toshigami]]''' (年神) or Ōtoshi (大年神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) # '''[[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂神, Uka-no-mitama-no-kami), commonly known as [[:en:Inari Ōkami|'''Inari''']] (稲荷神, Inari-no-kami)
* [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|'''Ko-no-hana-chiru-hime''']] (木花知流比売) - wife of Yashimajinumi (八島士奴美神), his nephew through Susanoo * '''[[Iwanaga-hime]]''' (石長比売) or (磐長姫命 in the [[Kujiki|''Kujiki'' (旧事紀), or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (先代旧事本紀)]]) - sister of Ko-no-sakuya-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] * '''Ko-no-sakuya-hime''' (木花之佐久夜毘売) - sister of Iwanaga-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]
including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[:en:Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[:en:Goddess|kami]] of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[:en:Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Izumo generations == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Ooyamatsimu]][[sn:commons:Category:Ōyamatsumi]][[de:Ōyamatsumi]][[en:Ōyamatsumi]][[fa:اویاماتسومی]][[fr:Ōyamatsumi]][[hr:O-Yama-Tsu-mi]][[ja:オオヤマツミ]][[ko:오야마쓰미노카미]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ]][[ru:Ооямацуми]][[simple:Ōyamatsumi]][[sv:Ōyamatsumi]][[zh:大山津見]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386563]] Wikidata name: Ōyamatsumi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese god}} {{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=July 2019}} {{Cleanup rewrite|date=December 2023}} }} __FORCETOC__ {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[:en:Konohana Sakuya|Konohana Sakuya]] | siblings = [[Kaya-no-hime]] | caption = [[Kamiumi]] from the [[Kojiki]] | image =File:Ooyamatsumi shinbutsu-zue.jpg| greek_equivalent = [[:en:Ourea|Ourea]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents ={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}| consort = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | offspring = {{ill|Iwanaga|WD=Q8191705}}; {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}}; {{ill|Konohanachiruhime|WD=Q48745675}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} | venerated_in = {{ill|Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|WD=Q127948103}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|mountain|WD=Q8502}} }} '''Ōyama-tsumi''' or '''Ohoyama-tsumi''' ([[Kojiki]]: {{lang|ja|大山津見神}} or [[Nihon Shoki]]: {{lang|ja|大山祇神}}, {{lang|ja|大山積神}}, {{lang|ja|大山罪神}}), also '''Ōyama-tsumi-mi'oya-no-mikoto''' ({{lang|ja|大山祇御祖命}}), is a god of mountains, sea, and war in [[Japanese mythology]]. He is an elder brother of [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. His other names are '''Watashi-no-Ōkami''' ({{lang|ja|和多志大神}}) and '''Sakatoke''' ({{lang|ja|酒解神}}).
== Genealogy == In the [[Kamiumi]] of the [[Kojiki]], Ōyama-tsumi was born between [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. After which he gave birth with '''[[Kaya-no-hime]]''' (鹿屋野比売神), also known as '''No-zuchi''' (野椎神), female deity from their union, the following gods pairs of eight were born:
* '''Ame-no-sazuchi''' (天之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sazuchi''' (国之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-sagiri''' (天之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sagiri''' (国之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-kurado''' (天之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-kurado''' (国之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ohoto-mato-hiko''' (大戸惑子神), male deity * '''Ohoto-mato-hime''' (大戸惑女神), female deity
Other children of Ōyama-tsumi who are parentless without a mother:
* '''Kamu'ō-ichi-hime''' (神大市比売), her children:
# '''Ōtoshi''' (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) or Nigihayahi (饒速日尊 Nigihayahi-no-mikoto), commonly known: '''[[Toshigami]]''' (年神) or Ōtoshi (大年神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) # '''[[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂神, Uka-no-mitama-no-kami), commonly known as [[:en:Inari Ōkami|'''Inari''']] (稲荷神, Inari-no-kami)
* [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|'''Ko-no-hana-chiru-hime''']] (木花知流比売) - wife of Yashimajinumi (八島士奴美神), his nephew through Susanoo * '''[[Iwanaga-hime]]''' (石長比売) or (磐長姫命 in the [[Kujiki|''Kujiki'' (旧事紀), or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (先代旧事本紀)]]) - sister of Ko-no-sakuya-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] * '''Ko-no-sakuya-hime''' (木花之佐久夜毘売) - sister of Iwanaga-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]
including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[:en:Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[:en:Goddess|kami]] of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[:en:Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Izumo generations == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Ooyamatsimu]][[sn:commons:Category:Ōyamatsumi]][[de:Ōyamatsumi]][[en:Ōyamatsumi]][[fa:اویاماتسومی]][[fr:Ōyamatsumi]][[hr:O-Yama-Tsu-mi]][[ja:オオヤマツミ]][[ko:오야마쓰미노카미]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ]][[ru:Ооямацуми]][[simple:Ōyamatsumi]][[sv:Ōyamatsumi]][[zh:大山津見]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386563]] Wikidata name: Ōyamatsumi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese god}} {{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=July 2019}} {{Cleanup rewrite|date=December 2023}} }} __FORCETOC__ {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[:en:Konohana Sakuya|Konohana Sakuya]] | siblings = [[Kaya-no-hime]] | caption = [[Kamiumi]] from the [[Kojiki]] | image =File:Ooyamatsumi shinbutsu-zue.jpg| greek_equivalent = [[:en:Ourea|Ourea]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents ={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}| consort = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | offspring = {{ill|Iwanaga|WD=Q8191705}}; {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}}; {{ill|Konohanachiruhime|WD=Q48745675}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} | venerated_in = {{ill|Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|WD=Q127948103}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|mountain|WD=Q8502}} }} '''Ōyama-tsumi''' or '''Ohoyama-tsumi''' ([[Kojiki]]: {{lang|ja|大山津見神}} or [[Nihon Shoki]]: {{lang|ja|大山祇神}}, {{lang|ja|大山積神}}, {{lang|ja|大山罪神}}), also '''Ōyama-tsumi-mi'oya-no-mikoto''' ({{lang|ja|大山祇御祖命}}), is a god of mountains, sea, and war in [[Japanese mythology]]. He is an elder brother of [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. His other names are '''Watashi-no-Ōkami''' ({{lang|ja|和多志大神}}) and '''Sakatoke''' ({{lang|ja|酒解神}}).
== Genealogy == In the [[Kamiumi]] of the [[Kojiki]], Ōyama-tsumi was born between [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. After which he gave birth with '''[[Kaya-no-hime]]''' (鹿屋野比売神), also known as '''No-zuchi''' (野椎神), female deity from their union, the following gods pairs of eight were born:
* '''Ame-no-sazuchi''' (天之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sazuchi''' (国之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-sagiri''' (天之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sagiri''' (国之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-kurado''' (天之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-kurado''' (国之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ohoto-mato-hiko''' (大戸惑子神), male deity * '''Ohoto-mato-hime''' (大戸惑女神), female deity
Other children of Ōyama-tsumi who are parentless without a mother:
* '''Kamu'ō-ichi-hime''' (神大市比売), her children:
# '''Ōtoshi''' (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) or Nigihayahi (饒速日尊 Nigihayahi-no-mikoto), commonly known: '''[[Toshigami]]''' (年神) or Ōtoshi (大年神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) # '''[[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂神, Uka-no-mitama-no-kami), commonly known as [[:en:Inari Ōkami|'''Inari''']] (稲荷神, Inari-no-kami)
* [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|'''Ko-no-hana-chiru-hime''']] (木花知流比売) - wife of Yashimajinumi (八島士奴美神), his nephew through Susanoo * '''[[Iwanaga-hime]]''' (石長比売) or (磐長姫命 in the [[Kujiki|''Kujiki'' (旧事紀), or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (先代旧事本紀)]]) - sister of Ko-no-sakuya-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] * '''Ko-no-sakuya-hime''' (木花之佐久夜毘売) - sister of Iwanaga-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]
including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[:en:Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[:en:Goddess|kami]] of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[:en:Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Izumo generations == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Ooyamatsimu]][[sn:commons:Category:Ōyamatsumi]][[de:Ōyamatsumi]][[en:Ōyamatsumi]][[fa:اویاماتسومی]][[fr:Ōyamatsumi]][[hr:O-Yama-Tsu-mi]][[ja:オオヤマツミ]][[ko:오야마쓰미노카미]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ]][[ru:Ооямацуми]][[simple:Ōyamatsumi]][[sv:Ōyamatsumi]][[zh:大山津見]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386563]] Wikidata name: Ōyamatsumi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese god}} {{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=July 2019}} {{Cleanup rewrite|date=December 2023}} }} __FORCETOC__ {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[:en:Konohana Sakuya|Konohana Sakuya]] | siblings = [[Kaya-no-hime]] | caption = [[Kamiumi]] from the [[Kojiki]] | image =File:Ooyamatsumi shinbutsu-zue.jpg| greek_equivalent = [[:en:Ourea|Ourea]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents ={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}| consort = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | offspring = {{ill|Iwanaga|WD=Q8191705}}; {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}}; {{ill|Konohanachiruhime|WD=Q48745675}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} | venerated_in = {{ill|Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|WD=Q127948103}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|mountain|WD=Q8502}} }} '''Ōyama-tsumi''' or '''Ohoyama-tsumi''' ([[Kojiki]]: {{lang|ja|大山津見神}} or [[Nihon Shoki]]: {{lang|ja|大山祇神}}, {{lang|ja|大山積神}}, {{lang|ja|大山罪神}}), also '''Ōyama-tsumi-mi'oya-no-mikoto''' ({{lang|ja|大山祇御祖命}}), is a god of mountains, sea, and war in [[Japanese mythology]]. He is an elder brother of [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. His other names are '''Watashi-no-Ōkami''' ({{lang|ja|和多志大神}}) and '''Sakatoke''' ({{lang|ja|酒解神}}).
== Genealogy == In the [[Kamiumi]] of the [[Kojiki]], Ōyama-tsumi was born between [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. After which he gave birth with '''[[Kaya-no-hime]]''' (鹿屋野比売神), also known as '''No-zuchi''' (野椎神), female deity from their union, the following gods pairs of eight were born:
* '''Ame-no-sazuchi''' (天之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sazuchi''' (国之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-sagiri''' (天之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sagiri''' (国之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-kurado''' (天之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-kurado''' (国之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ohoto-mato-hiko''' (大戸惑子神), male deity * '''Ohoto-mato-hime''' (大戸惑女神), female deity
Other children of Ōyama-tsumi who are parentless without a mother:
* '''Kamu'ō-ichi-hime''' (神大市比売), her children:
# '''Ōtoshi''' (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) or Nigihayahi (饒速日尊 Nigihayahi-no-mikoto), commonly known: '''[[Toshigami]]''' (年神) or Ōtoshi (大年神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) # '''[[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂神, Uka-no-mitama-no-kami), commonly known as [[:en:Inari Ōkami|'''Inari''']] (稲荷神, Inari-no-kami)
* [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|'''Ko-no-hana-chiru-hime''']] (木花知流比売) - wife of Yashimajinumi (八島士奴美神), his nephew through Susanoo * '''[[Iwanaga-hime]]''' (石長比売) or (磐長姫命 in the [[Kujiki|''Kujiki'' (旧事紀), or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (先代旧事本紀)]]) - sister of Ko-no-sakuya-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] * '''Ko-no-sakuya-hime''' (木花之佐久夜毘売) - sister of Iwanaga-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]
including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[:en:Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[:en:Goddess|kami]] of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[:en:Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Izumo generations == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Ooyamatsimu]][[sn:commons:Category:Ōyamatsumi]][[de:Ōyamatsumi]][[en:Ōyamatsumi]][[fa:اویاماتسومی]][[fr:Ōyamatsumi]][[hr:O-Yama-Tsu-mi]][[ja:オオヤマツミ]][[ko:오야마쓰미노카미]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ]][[ru:Ооямацуми]][[simple:Ōyamatsumi]][[sv:Ōyamatsumi]][[zh:大山津見]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386563]] Wikidata name: Ōyamatsumi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese god}} {{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=July 2019}} {{Cleanup rewrite|date=December 2023}} }} __FORCETOC__ {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[:en:Konohana Sakuya|Konohana Sakuya]] | siblings = [[Kaya-no-hime]] | caption = [[Kamiumi]] from the [[Kojiki]] | image =File:Ooyamatsumi shinbutsu-zue.jpg| greek_equivalent = [[:en:Ourea|Ourea]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents ={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}| consort = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | offspring = {{ill|Iwanaga|WD=Q8191705}}; {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}}; {{ill|Konohanachiruhime|WD=Q48745675}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} | venerated_in = {{ill|Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|WD=Q127948103}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|mountain|WD=Q8502}} }} '''Ōyama-tsumi''' or '''Ohoyama-tsumi''' ([[Kojiki]]: {{lang|ja|大山津見神}} or [[Nihon Shoki]]: {{lang|ja|大山祇神}}, {{lang|ja|大山積神}}, {{lang|ja|大山罪神}}), also '''Ōyama-tsumi-mi'oya-no-mikoto''' ({{lang|ja|大山祇御祖命}}), is a god of mountains, sea, and war in [[Japanese mythology]]. He is an elder brother of [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. His other names are '''Watashi-no-Ōkami''' ({{lang|ja|和多志大神}}) and '''Sakatoke''' ({{lang|ja|酒解神}}).
== Genealogy == In the [[Kamiumi]] of the [[Kojiki]], Ōyama-tsumi was born between [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. After which he gave birth with '''[[Kaya-no-hime]]''' (鹿屋野比売神), also known as '''No-zuchi''' (野椎神), female deity from their union, the following gods pairs of eight were born:
* '''Ame-no-sazuchi''' (天之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sazuchi''' (国之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-sagiri''' (天之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sagiri''' (国之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-kurado''' (天之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-kurado''' (国之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ohoto-mato-hiko''' (大戸惑子神), male deity * '''Ohoto-mato-hime''' (大戸惑女神), female deity
Other children of Ōyama-tsumi who are parentless without a mother:
* '''Kamu'ō-ichi-hime''' (神大市比売), her children:
# '''Ōtoshi''' (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) or Nigihayahi (饒速日尊 Nigihayahi-no-mikoto), commonly known: '''[[Toshigami]]''' (年神) or Ōtoshi (大年神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) # '''[[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂神, Uka-no-mitama-no-kami), commonly known as [[:en:Inari Ōkami|'''Inari''']] (稲荷神, Inari-no-kami)
* [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|'''Ko-no-hana-chiru-hime''']] (木花知流比売) - wife of Yashimajinumi (八島士奴美神), his nephew through Susanoo * '''[[Iwanaga-hime]]''' (石長比売) or (磐長姫命 in the [[Kujiki|''Kujiki'' (旧事紀), or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (先代旧事本紀)]]) - sister of Ko-no-sakuya-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] * '''Ko-no-sakuya-hime''' (木花之佐久夜毘売) - sister of Iwanaga-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]
including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[:en:Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[:en:Goddess|kami]] of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[:en:Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}

including the twin deities:

[[en:Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Ōyamatsumi}} [[de:Ōyamatsumi]] [[fa:اویاماتسومی]] [[fr:Ōyamatsumi]] [[hr:O-Yama-Tsu-mi]] [[ja:オオヤマツミ]] [[ko:오야마쓰미노카미]] [[mni:ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ]] [[ru:Ооямацуми]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[simple:Ōyamatsumi]] [[sv:Ōyamatsumi]] [[zh:大山津見]] {{wikidata link|Q386563}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Ooyamatsimu]][[sn:commons:Category:Ōyamatsumi]][[de:Ōyamatsumi]][[en:Ōyamatsumi]][[fa:اویاماتسومی]][[fr:Ōyamatsumi]][[hr:O-Yama-Tsu-mi]][[ja:オオヤマツミ]][[ko:오야마쓰미노카미]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ]][[ru:Ооямацуми]][[simple:Ōyamatsumi]][[sv:Ōyamatsumi]][[zh:大山津見]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386563]] Wikidata name: Ōyamatsumi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese god}} {{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=July 2019}} {{Cleanup rewrite|date=December 2023}} }} __FORCETOC__ {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[:en:Konohana Sakuya|Konohana Sakuya]] | siblings = [[Kaya-no-hime]] | caption = [[Kamiumi]] from the [[Kojiki]] | image =File:Ooyamatsumi shinbutsu-zue.jpg| greek_equivalent = [[:en:Ourea|Ourea]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents ={{ill|Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}| consort = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | offspring = {{ill|Iwanaga|WD=Q8191705}}; {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}}; {{ill|Konohanachiruhime|WD=Q48745675}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} | venerated_in = {{ill|Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|WD=Q127948103}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|mountain|WD=Q8502}} }} '''Ōyama-tsumi''' or '''Ohoyama-tsumi''' ([[Kojiki]]: {{lang|ja|大山津見神}} or [[Nihon Shoki]]: {{lang|ja|大山祇神}}, {{lang|ja|大山積神}}, {{lang|ja|大山罪神}}), also '''Ōyama-tsumi-mi'oya-no-mikoto''' ({{lang|ja|大山祇御祖命}}), is a god of mountains, sea, and war in [[Japanese mythology]]. He is an elder brother of [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. His other names are '''Watashi-no-Ōkami''' ({{lang|ja|和多志大神}}) and '''Sakatoke''' ({{lang|ja|酒解神}}).
== Genealogy == In the [[Kamiumi]] of the [[Kojiki]], Ōyama-tsumi was born between [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. After which he gave birth with '''[[Kaya-no-hime]]''' (鹿屋野比売神), also known as '''No-zuchi''' (野椎神), female deity from their union, the following gods pairs of eight were born:
* '''Ame-no-sazuchi''' (天之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sazuchi''' (国之狭土神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-sagiri''' (天之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-sagiri''' (国之狭霧神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ame-no-kurado''' (天之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Kuni-no-kurado''' (国之闇戸神), genderless deity and spirit * '''Ohoto-mato-hiko''' (大戸惑子神), male deity * '''Ohoto-mato-hime''' (大戸惑女神), female deity
Other children of Ōyama-tsumi who are parentless without a mother:
* '''Kamu'ō-ichi-hime''' (神大市比売), her children:
# '''Ōtoshi''' (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) or Nigihayahi (饒速日尊 Nigihayahi-no-mikoto), commonly known: '''[[Toshigami]]''' (年神) or Ōtoshi (大年神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) # '''[[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂神, Uka-no-mitama-no-kami), commonly known as [[:en:Inari Ōkami|'''Inari''']] (稲荷神, Inari-no-kami)
* [[:en:Konohanachiru-hime|'''Ko-no-hana-chiru-hime''']] (木花知流比売) - wife of Yashimajinumi (八島士奴美神), his nephew through Susanoo * '''[[Iwanaga-hime]]''' (石長比売) or (磐長姫命 in the [[Kujiki|''Kujiki'' (旧事紀), or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (先代旧事本紀)]]) - sister of Ko-no-sakuya-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] * '''Ko-no-sakuya-hime''' (木花之佐久夜毘売) - sister of Iwanaga-hime and wife of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]]
including the twin deities:
* '''Ashinazuchi''' or '''Ashinadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 足名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 脚摩乳命) - other names: (足摩乳命, 足名槌命 Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi -no-mikoto; 足名鉄神, Ashinazuchi or Ashinadzuchi) * '''Tenazuchi''' or '''Tenadzuchi''' ([[Kojiki]]: 手名椎; [[Nihon Shoki]]: 手摩乳命) - other name: (名槌命, Tenazuchi or Tenadzuchi)
However, in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Ōyama-tsumi is supposed to be born when Izanagi slashed his child, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] (軻遇突智).
The child of Ōyama-tsumi from his first wife Kaya-no-hime, the deity '''Ame-no-sagiri''' has a daughter, '''Tohotsumachi-ne''' (遠津待根神), and the eighth descendant of the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]] (大国主神), the male deity '''Ame-no-hibara-ōshinadomi''' (天日腹大科度美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity, '''To'otsuyama-sakitarashi''' (遠津山岬多良斯神), who is the descendant of Ōyama-tsumi.
There is not much written about Ōyama-tsumi, and children associated with him appears at times. As for the myth of [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata-no-orochi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'s wife, [[Kushinadahime|Kushinada-hime]] (櫛名田比売命 Kushinada-hime-no-mikoto), and her twin parents, the male deity '''Ashinazuchi''' (足名椎) and female deity '''Tenazuchi''' (手名椎), are known and claimed to be the children of Ōyama-tsumi.
Afterwards, the lineage falls together with his descendants of his half-brother Susanoo, with the union of Ōyama-tsumi first daughter, Kamu'ō-ichi-hime (神大市比売), between them gave birth to [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi (or Toshigami)]] and [[Ukanomitama|Uka-no-mitama]]. Then, Susanoo's union with Ōyama-tsumi granddaughter, Kushinada-hime, gave birth to the male deity '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神). Then Yashimajinumi married Ōyama-tsumi's daughter '''Konohachiru-hime''' (木花知流比売), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fuwanomojikunusunu/ Fuwanomodjikunusunu''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, or '''Fuhanomojikunusunu/ Fuhanomodjikunusunu'''). Fuwanomojikunusu is the husband of '''Hikawa-hime''' (日河比売, or '''Hikaha-hime'''), the daughter of the male deity [[Kuraokami|Okami]] (淤加美神), from their union gave birth to the male deity '''Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana''' (深淵之水夜礼花神).
Then, Fukafuchi-no-Mizuyarehana married '''Ame-no-tsudohechine''' (天之都度閇知泥神, or 阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼, '''Ama-no-tsutohenochine''' in the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記)) and from their union gave birth to '''Omizunu/ Omidzunu''' (淤美豆奴神, or 意弥都奴). Then, Omizunu/ Omidzunu married the female deity '''Futemimi''' (布帝耳神), who is the daughter of the deity '''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神) and from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] (天之冬衣神). Ame-no-Fuyukinu married the female deity {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}} (刺国若比売), who is the daughter of the male deity {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Ōkami|lt=Sashikuni Okami|lt=Sashikuni|ja|刺国大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Okami|qq=}} (刺国大神, '''Sashikuni Ōkami'''), from their union gave birth to the male deity [[Ōkuninushi]], the sixth generation grandson of Ōyama-tsumi. These are recorded from a book called the "Origin of the Great Shining Deity ''Awaga''" (''Awaga Daimyōjin Mototsufumi,'' 粟鹿大明神元記) at Awaga Shrine (粟鹿神社, Awaga Jinja) in Awaga, town of [[:en:Santō, Hyōgo|Santō]], [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
In the myth of '''''[[Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]]''''' (天孫降臨), the descent of [[Amaterasu]]'s grandson [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] from Heaven ([[Takamagahara]]) to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], Ninigi has encountered Ōyama-tsumi's daughter [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime]] (木花之佐久夜毘売),<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 71. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref> - the [[:en:Goddess|kami]] of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]], Ōyama-tsumi has offered both Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime and her older sister Iwanaga-hime (石長比売). Then, when Ninigi sends back Iwanaga-hime only by her ugly appearance, which in return has angered Ōyama-tsumi and said, "The reason why I gave together with Iwanaga-hime aside from her sibling Ko-no-hanasakuya-hime was because I made a pledge that the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) would be eternal like a rock, but she was sent back and the life expectancy of the heavenly grandson (Ninigi) was shortened."
His most important shrine, [[Ōyamazumi Shrine]], is located on [[:en:Ōmishima, Ehime|Ōmishima]].{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}}
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Izumo generations == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
